original: Cardinal Ugolino di Conti is elected Pope Gregory IX
Mongolian victory at the naval Battle of Yamen ends the Song Dynasty in China
Frederick III of Hapsburg crowned Holy Roman Emperor by Pope Nicholas V at St. Peter's Basilica in Rome -last Holy Emperor crowned in Rome
Giovanni de Varrazano, a Florentine explorer in the service of King Francis I of France, lands around area of Carolinas
Court of Holland names Amsterdam sheriff John Hubrechtsz a "heretic"
Peace of Amboise: Rights for Huguenots
Spanish troops occupy Manila
Assembly of the French clergy issues a declaration stating, among other things, that the power of the King is not subject to papal authority
Explorer René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle, searching for the mouth of the Mississippi River, is murdered by his own men
British Parliament passes the Plantation Act, naturalizing foreign Protestants and others residing for seven years or more in any of Britain's American colonies
Four people are buried by an avalanche for 37 days in Bergemoletto, Italy; three survive
Poland and Prussia sign trade agreement
Friedrich Schiller's "Die Braut von Messina" premieres in Weimar
The first Spanish constitution is enacted, one of the earliest constitutions ever promulgated
The First Taranaki War ends in New Zealand

Confederate cruiser SS Georgiana is destroyed on her maiden voyage with a cargo of munitions and medicines valued at over $1,000,000. The wreck is discovered exactly 102 years later by teenage diver and pioneer underwater archaeologist E. Lee Spence.
Charles Gounod's opera "Mireille" premieres at the Théâtre Lyrique in Paris, France
Battle of Bentonville, Confederates retreat from Greenville North Carolina
Immigrant ship Monarch of the Seas sinks in Liverpool; 738 die
Antônio Carlos Gomes' opera "Il Guarany" premieres at La Scala Teatro in Milan, Kingdom of Italy
Australia beat England by 45 runs in very first Test match
3 brothers Hearne play in same Test Cricket England v South Africa (Cape Town)
Los Angeles Railway is established to provide streetcar service
18.8 cm precipitation at Lewer's Ranch, Nevada (state record)

1st International Women's Day sees over 1 million men and women attend rallies in Austria, Denmark, Germany and Switzerland. Issues discussed included women's right to vote and to hold public office, the right to work, to vocational training and an end to discrimination on the job.
Stanley Cup, Arena Gardens, Toronto ON: Toronto HC (NHA) defeat Victoria Aristocrats (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-0 series sweep; final series of the "challenge" era
Pluto photographed for 1st time (although unknown at the time)
VI Summer (Modern) Olympic Games: IOC President Pierre de Coubertin writes to Associated Press indicating 1916 Berlin Games won't take place because of WWI
US Supreme Court uphoelds 8-hr work day for railroad employees
Victor Herbert and Harry Blossom's operetta "Eileen", loosely based on a novel by Herbert's grandfather, premieres at Shubert Theater, New York City
S Potter becomes 1st US pilot to shoot down a German seaplane
US adopts Standard Time Act of 1918, also known as the Calder Act, a federal law implementing standard time (and daylight saving time) and defining five time zones for the United States
Italian Fascists shoot from the Parenzana train at a group of children in Strunjan (Slovenia): two children are killed, two mangled and three wounded
Bloody battles between communists & Nazis in Berlin
"Amos & Andy" debuts on radio (NBC Blue Network-WMAQ Chicago)
Nakagawa Soen accepted as a student of Katsube Keigaku Roshi
Nevada legalizes gambling
Sydney Harbour Bridge opens in Sydney, Australia
Astronomer Fritz Zwicky publishes his research on stellar explosions, coining the term "supernova" and hypothesizing that they are the origin of cosmic rays
NHL Toronto Maple Leafs and New York Americans combine to score 8 goals in just under 5 minutes; Toronto wins game 8-5
Failed British air raid on German base at Sylt
Jimmy Dorsey & his Orchestra record "Green Eyes" and "Maria Elena", with vocals by Helen O'Connell and Bob Eberly, in New York City
Thoroughbred Racing Association of US formed in Chicago
British 8th army opens assault on Mareth line, Tunisia
Frank Nitti, the Chicago Outfit Boss after Al Capone, commits suicide at the Chicago Central Railyard
British composer Michael Tippett's oratorio "A Child of Our Time" premieres at the Adelphi Theatre in London, England
800 killed as Kamikaze attacked USS Franklin off Japan
British 36th division conquers Mogok (ruby mine)
US Task Force 58 attacks ships near Kobe/Kure
French Guyana, Guadeloupe, Martinique & Reunion become overseas "departments" of France
Nikolai Mikhailovich Schwernik succeeds Kalinin as Chairman of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet of the USSR
Belgian government of Paul-Henri Spaak (Belgium Socialist Party) forms
Chinese Nationalist GeneralChiang Kai-shekconquers Jinan
Lee Savold KOs Gino Buonvino in 54 seconds at Madison Square Garden, NYC
1st museum devoted exclusively to atomic energy, Oak Ridge, Tennessee
City College of NY defeats Bradley to win the NIT
Herman Wouk's novel "The Caine Mutiny" published (Pulitzer Prize 1952)
1st color telecast of a prize fight, Giardello vs Troy in Madison Square Garden, NYC
1st rocket-driven sled on rails tested in Alamogordo, NM
Biggest NBA margin of victory - Minn Lakers-133, St Louis Hawks-75
MLB Cleveland Indians reject Boston Red Sox offer of $1 million for pitcher Herb Score
Archbishop Suenens of Mechelen-Brussels appointed cardinal
Indonesia nationalizes all foreign oil companies
Rembrandt's "Titus" sells for then record 7,770,000 guilders
Stoica becomes president & Ceausescu party leader of Romania

The wreck of the SS Georgiana, valued at over $50,000,000 and said to be the most powerful Confederate cruiser, is discovered by teenage diver and pioneer underwater archaeologist E. Lee Spence exactly 102 years after its destruction
Belgium government of Vanden Boeynants begins
French Somaliland (Djibouti) votes to continue association with France
Howard University, Washington, D.C., students seize administration building
British invade Anguilla
Chicago 8 indicted in aftermath of Chicago Democratic convention
The 385 metre tall TV-mast at Emley Moor, United Kingdom, collapses due to ice build- up.
W German chancellor & E German premier meet
Philadelphia 76ers outscore Cincinnati Royals 90-80 in 2nd half enroute to a 147-127 victory
1st AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Immaculata beats West Chester State 52-48 in Normal
India & Bangladesh sign friendship treaty
LA Lakers beat Golden State Warriors, 162-99, by then record 63 pts
Jefferson Starship begins their 1st tour
Pennsylvania is 1st state to allow girls to compete with boys in HS sports
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Jevgeni Kulikov skates world record 1000m (1:15.33)
50,000 demonstrate in Amsterdam against neutron bomb
US House of Representatives begins live TV broadcasts via C-SPAN
2 workers killed in space shuttle Columbia accident
Buffalo Sabres beat Toronto Maple Leafs 14-4
Falklands War: Argentinian forces land on South Georgia Island, precipitating war with the U.K.
National Guard jet tanker crashes killing 27
John J O'Connor named 8th archbishop of New York
KSD-AM in St Louis MO changes call letters to KUSA
Mobil oil tanker spills 200,000 gallons into Columbia River
STS 41-C vehicle moves to launch pad
"Spin Magazine" begins publishing
US Senate votes 55-45 to authorize production of the MX "Peacekeeper" intercontinental ballistic missile
Fred Currey acquires Greyhound Bus Company
Hassanali inaugurated as president of Trinidad & Tobago
Two British Army corporals are attacked during a funeral procession, beaten and shot dead by the Provisional IRA in Belfast, North Ireland
Yvonne van Gennipskates un-official world record 10 km (15:25.25)
Boeing V-22 Osprey VTOL aircraft makes maiden flight
Ice Dance Championship at Paris won by M Klimova & S Ponomarenko (USSR)
Ice Pairs World Championship at Paris won by Ekaterina Gordeeva & Sergei Grinkov (USSR)
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champ in Paris won by Midori Ito (Japan)
1st world ice hockey tournament for women held (Ottawa)
Sacramento Kings set NBA record of 29 consecutive road losses
Britain's Prince Andrew & Sarah, Duchess of York, announce separation
Ice Dance Championship in Prague won by M Usova & A Zhulin (RUS)
Ice Pairs Championship in Prague won by I Brasseur & L Eisler (CAN)
US Supreme Court Justice Byron R White announced plans to retire
2,500 kilograms of cocaine intercepted in Zeewolde, Netherlands
Largest omelette (1,383sq ft) made with 160,000 eggs in Yokohama, Japan
NJ Devils club record 41st win of the season
AmericanBonnie Blairskates female world record point total (156.450)
Arizona begins using new telephone area code 520, outside of Phoenix
Finnish Social Democratic Party wins parliamentary election
NBA NY Knicks beat NY Nets in 100th meeting (Knicks 53 Nets 47)
Neil Marshall skates world record 3 km (3:54.08)
Ice Pairs won by Mandy Woetzel & Ingo Steuer (GER)
Major League Baseball announces 5 year/$50M deal with Pepsi
US Supreme Court hears Internet indecency arguments
Bank of Japan issues a monetary policy known as quantitative easing, which stimulates the Japanese economy after the burst of the dot-com bubble
U.S. invasion of Afghanistan: Operation Anaconda ends (started on March 2) after killing 500 Taliban and al Qaeda fighters with 11 allied troop fatalities
A Swedish DC-3 shot down by a Russian MiG-15 in the 1950s is finally recovered after years of work. The remains of the crew are left in place, pending further investigations.
A truck and a bus crash head-on in Äänekoski, Finland. 24 people are killed and 13 injured.
Taiwanese president Chen Shui-bian is shot just before the country's presidential election on March 20.
GRB 080319B: A cosmic burst that is the farthest object visible to the naked eye is briefly observed
Wendy's overtakes Burger King to become the second best-selling hamburger chain
16 people are killed by mudslides in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
27 people are killed and 14 are injured in a bus crash in Maharashtra, India
NASA's Mars rover Curiosity discovers further evidence of water-bearing minerals
Israel sends airforce against Syrian military units involved in an attack yesterday on an Israeli patrol in the Golan Heights
Russia captures the Ukrainian naval base in Sevastopol
Mississippi signs into law the US's strictest abortion laws, no termination after 15 weeks
World's last male northern white rhino, 45-year-old Sudan, dies in Kenya
"Superbloom" of poppies in Walker Canyon, southern California visible from space, after high rainfall
American Karen Uhlenbeck becomes the 1st woman to win mathematics Abel Prize
Houston Rockets guard James Harden becomes first player in NBA history to score at least 30 points against all 29 opponents in a single season with 31 in a 121-105 win over the Atlanta Hawks
Image of Australian rules footballer Tayla Harris attacked by internet trolls and controversially removed before being reinstated
Singer Sam Smith comes out as non-binary in interview on Jameela Jamil’s Instagram show
Italian death toll from COVID-19 at 3,405 surpasses China's official total (3,245) making it then the worst-affected country in the world
Professional football in England is further postponed until 'no earlier than 30 April' due to the COVID-19 pandemic
State of California with 910 cases of COVID-19 locks down and orders people to "stay at home"
Icelandic volcano Fagradalsfjall erupts for the first time in 800 years and after more than 50,000 earthquakes
UBS, Switzerland's largest bank agrees to buy its rival Credit Suisse (established 1856) for about $3.2 billion to help ease global financial panic, in deal brokered by Swiss government
China launches Queqiao-2 relay satellite for the far side of the moon to support its Chang’e-6 lunar sample return mission
Finland is ranked the happiest country in the world by the UN for the seventh year in a row, with Afghanistan the lowest ranked
Hong Kong parliament passes tough new security law Article 23 focusing on sentences for external interference and insurrection
New data on dark energy from the DESI telescope further upends scientific belief that the universe is constantly expanding, its results show it has instead weakened over the last 4.5 million years
Norman monk Gundulf consecrated as Bishop of Rochester - goes on to build Rochester Castle and the White Tower, Tower of London, as engineer ofWilliam the Conqueror
Two hundred members of the Peking imperial family and court commit suicide out of loyalty to the last Ming Emperor,Chongzhen
Joseph Haydn's oratorio "The Creation" ("Die Schöpfung") first public performance premieres at the Burgtheater in Vienna, Austria
Charles IV, king of Spain, abdicates after riots and a popular revolt at the winter palace Aranjuez. His son,Ferdinand VII, takes the throne
Charles Gounod's opera "Faust" premieres at the Théâtre Lyrique in Paris, France
First stone is laid for theSagrada Familiabasilica in Barcelona, Spain, designed by Antoni Gaudí
Louis Rielreturns to Canada, proclaims provisional government in Saskatchewan
Antonín Dvořák's Cello Concerto in B minor, Op. 104, premieres in London with English cellist Leo Stern as soloist
Ermanno Wolf-Ferrari's comic opera "Quattro Rusteghi" ("The Four Ruffians") premieres at the Hoftheater in Munich, Germany
Literary Magazine "Littérature", edited byAndré Breton, Philippe Soupault, and Louis Aragon publishes its first issue
Angelo Giuseppe Roncalli (future PopeJohn XXIII) becomes a bishop, appointed as Apostolic Visitor to Bulgaria
PopePius XIpublishes encyclical Divini redemptoris against communism
French government ofÉdouard Daladierfalls
FDRorders men between 45 & 64 to register for non-military duty
In their third match,Jake LaMottafinally defeats Jimmy Reeves by knocking him out in the sixth round
Adolf Hitlerissues "Nero Decree" to destroy all German factories
25th Academy Awards: "Greatest Show on Earth",Gary Cooper&Shirley Boothwin (1st time televised)
Tennessee Williams' "Camino Real" premieres in NYC
Britain's first planetarium opens atMadame Tussaud's, London
Garfield Soberscompletes a century in each innings for WI vs. Pakistan
Columbia Records releases the self-titled debut album of American folk singerBob Dylan; quickly recorded collection of mostly cover tunes, with two original songs is largely ignored
Sean Connery's 1st day of shooting on James Bond film "Goldfinger"
Last episode of "TheMary Tyler MooreShow" premieres on CBS, with a group hug searching for a box tissues, then singing "It's a Long Way to Tipperary"
American sitcom "Kate & Allie" premieres on CBS TV starring Susan Saint James andJane Curtin
Two-time Cy Young Award winnerDenny McLainis indicted on charges of drug trafficking, embezzlement and racketeering; 25 year sentence later quashed
American televangelistJim Bakkerresigns amid rape accusation by his secretary, Jessica Hahn
Bonnie Blairskates ladies' world record 500 m (39.43 sec)
Yvonne van Gennipskates ladies world record 3 km (4:16.85)
Gorbachevsays the Soviet Union will cut its oil exports by nearly half
MLB Kansas City Royals announce they are puttingBo Jacksonon waivers
NFL owners strip Phoenix of the 1993 Super Bowl game because Arizona does not recognize the federal holidayMartin Luther King Jr.Day
St Louis BluesBrett Hullis 3rd NHLer to score 80 goals in a season
Lara scores 167 for WI v England at Georgetown
Michael Jordanrejoins NBA Chicago Bulls after 17 months trying a  career in baseball; he scores 19 poimts in 103-96 loss to the Pacers in Indianapolis
16th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Aerosmith; Solomon Burke; The Flamingos;Michael Jackson; Queen;Paul Simon;Steely Dan; Ritchie Valens; James Burton; Johnnie Johnson; and Chris Blackwell
On the weekend of his 84th birthday,Fred Shuttlesworthgives his final sermon in front of 300 people at the Greater New Light Baptist Church
Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice, directed by Zack Snyder and starringBen AffleckandHenry Cavill, first premieres in Mexico City
Kazakh PresidentNursultan Nazarbayevannounces his resignation after nearly 30 years in office, the last soviet-era head of state
LeBron Jamesmoves pastKarl Malone(36,909) into second on NBA's all-time scoring list with 38 points in LA Lakers' 127-119 loss to Washington Wizards; Kareem Abdul-Jabbar leads (38,387)
PhilanthropistMacKenzie Scottannounces her organization Yield Giving is giving away $640 million to 361 nonprofits after an open call for applications
US Senate rejects the Treaty of Versailles for the second time, refusing to ratify the League of Nations' covenant and maintaining a policy of isolation
Airstrikes by an American and British-led coalition signal the beginning of the invasion of Iraq without United Nations support and in defiance of world opinion
